{{otheruses|Miwa Shrine in Tsubata, Kahoku District, Ishikawa Prefecture|other Miwa Shrines|{{ill|lt=Miwa Shrine (disambiguation)|UNKNOWN|lt=Miwa Shrine}}}} {{神社 |名称 = Miwa Shrine |画像 = [[画像:MIwajinja-tubata.jpg|250px]]
Torii |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Ishikawa Prefecture|Q131281|WD=Q131281}}{{ill|lt=Kahoku District|Q1130942|WD=Q1130942}}{{ill|lt=Tsubata|Q432014|WD=Q432014}} Aza Kitanakajō Sa-no-2-no-2 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi|Q867534|WD=Q867534|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto}}, plus seven other deities |社格 = candidate for {{ill|lt=Shikinai Shōsha|Q134917287|WD=Q134917287}}, former {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} |創建 = tradition: {{ill|lt=836|Q24957|WD=Q24957}} (Jōwa 3) |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=April 15|Q2497|WD=Q2497}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Ishikawa|label=Miwa Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Miwa Shrine''' (みわじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Aza Kitanakajō, {{ill|lt=Tsubata|Q432014|WD=Q432014}}, {{ill|lt=Kahoku District|Q1130942|WD=Q1130942}}, {{ill|lt=Ishikawa Prefecture|Q131281|WD=Q131281}}. It stands in the hills behind {{ill|lt=Tsubata Station|Q3701490|WD=Q3701490}}. Its {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=former shrine rank|1=UNKNOWN}} was {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. === Enshrined deities === It enshrines eight deities: {{ill|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi|Q867534|WD=Q867534|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Kukurihime|Q11619891|WD=Q11619891|lt=Kukurihime-no-Kami}}, {{ill|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi|Q21391580|WD=Q21391580|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q2564508}}, {{ill|lt=Himegami|Q22070227|WD=Q22070227}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, and {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10935105|lt=Ōyamakui-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}. === Origin === Its founding is said to be {{ill|lt=836|Q24957|WD=Q24957}} ({{ill|lt=Jōwa (Japan)|Q1075605|WD=Q1075605|lt=Jōwa}} 3), and it is considered the minor shrine "Miwa Shrine" in Kaga District, {{ill|lt=Kaga Province|Q864166|WD=Q864166}}, in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}Another candidate {{ill|lt=ronsha|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904}} is {{ill|lt=Ishiura Shrine|Q11586806|WD=Q11586806}} in {{ill|lt=Kanazawa|Q191130|WD=Q191130}}.. There is a pond in the precincts called Ryūjin-no-Ike. === Access === * 10 minutes on foot from Tsubata Station on {{ill|lt=West Japan Railway Company|Q502125|WD=Q502125}} (JR West) and {{ill|lt=IR Ishikawa Railway|Q11585936|WD=Q11585936}} === Notes === {{reflist}} ===References=== * Ishikawa Prefectural Shrine Office (ed.), ''Ishikawa-ken Jinja-shi'', Ishikawa Prefectural Shrine Office, October 26, 1976 === External links === *[https://www.ishikawa-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/j0931/ 三輪神社] (Ishikawa Prefectural Shrine Office) {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:みわしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Miwa Shrine (Q11357406)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Miwa Shrine (Q11357406)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kaga Province]] [[Category:石川県の神社]] [[Category:加賀国の式内社]] [[Category:石川県の旧村社]] [[Category:津幡町の建築物]] [[Category:津幡町の歴史]] [[Category:三輪神社]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QMiwa Shrine (Q11357406)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QMiwa Shrine (Q11357406)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Miwa Shrine (Q11357406)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

[[画像:MIwajinja-tubata.jpg|250px]]
Torii |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Ishikawa Prefecture|Q131281|WD=Q131281}}{{ill|lt=Kahoku District|Q1130942|WD=Q1130942}}{{ill|lt=Tsubata|Q432014|WD=Q432014}} Aza Kitanakajō Sa-no-2-no-2 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi|Q867534|WD=Q867534|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto}}, plus seven other deities |社格 = candidate for {{ill|lt=Shikinai Shōsha|Q134917287|WD=Q134917287}}, former {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} |創建 = tradition: {{ill|lt=836|Q24957|WD=Q24957}} (Jōwa 3) |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=April 15|Q2497|WD=Q2497}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Ishikawa|label=Miwa Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Miwa Shrine''' (みわじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Aza Kitanakajō, {{ill|lt=Tsubata|Q432014|WD=Q432014}}, {{ill|lt=Kahoku District|Q1130942|WD=Q1130942}}, {{ill|lt=Ishikawa Prefecture|Q131281|WD=Q131281}}. It stands in the hills behind {{ill|lt=Tsubata Station|Q3701490|WD=Q3701490}}. Its {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=former shrine rank|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q712534}} was {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. === Enshrined deities === It enshrines eight deities: {{ill|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi|Q867534|WD=Q867534|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Kukurihime|Q11619891|WD=Q11619891|lt=Kukurihime-no-Kami}}, {{ill|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi|Q21391580|WD=Q21391580|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q2564508}}, {{ill|lt=Himegami|Q22070227|WD=Q22070227}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, and {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10935105|lt=Ōyamakui-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}. === Origin === Its founding is said to be {{ill|lt=836|Q24957|WD=Q24957}} ({{ill|lt=Jōwa (Japan)|Q1075605|WD=Q1075605|lt=Jōwa}} 3), and it is considered the minor shrine "Miwa Shrine" in Kaga District, {{ill|lt=Kaga Province|Q864166|WD=Q864166}}, in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}Another candidate {{ill|lt=ronsha|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904}} is {{ill|lt=Ishiura Shrine|Q11586806|WD=Q11586806}} in {{ill|lt=Kanazawa|Q191130|WD=Q191130}}.. There is a pond in the precincts called Ryūjin-no-Ike. === Access === * 10 minutes on foot from Tsubata Station on {{ill|lt=West Japan Railway Company|Q502125|WD=Q502125}} (JR West) and {{ill|lt=IR Ishikawa Railway|Q11585936|WD=Q11585936}} === Notes === {{reflist}} ===References=== * Ishikawa Prefectural Shrine Office (ed.), ''Ishikawa-ken Jinja-shi'', Ishikawa Prefectural Shrine Office, October 26, 1976 === External links === *[https://www.ishikawa-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/j0931/ 三輪神社] (Ishikawa Prefectural Shrine Office) {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:みわしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Miwa Shrine (Q11357406)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Miwa Shrine (Q11357406)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kaga Province]] [[Category:石川県の神社]] [[Category:加賀国の式内社]] [[Category:石川県の旧村社]] [[Category:津幡町の建築物]] [[Category:津幡町の歴史]] [[Category:三輪神社]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QMiwa Shrine (Q11357406)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QMiwa Shrine (Q11357406)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Miwa Shrine (Q11357406)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

{{otheruses|Miwa Shrine in Tsubata, Kahoku District, Ishikawa Prefecture|other Miwa Shrines|{{ill|lt=Miwa Shrine (disambiguation)|UNKNOWN|lt=Miwa Shrine}}}} {{神社 |名称 = Miwa Shrine |画像 = [[画像:MIwajinja-tubata.jpg|250px]]
Torii |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Ishikawa Prefecture|Q131281|WD=Q131281}}{{ill|lt=Kahoku District|Q1130942|WD=Q1130942}}{{ill|lt=Tsubata|Q432014|WD=Q432014}} Aza Kitanakajō Sa-no-2-no-2 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi|Q867534|WD=Q867534|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto}}, plus seven other deities |社格 = candidate for {{ill|lt=Shikinai Shōsha|Q134917287|WD=Q134917287}}, former {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} |創建 = tradition: {{ill|lt=836|Q24957|WD=Q24957}} (Jōwa 3) |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=April 15|Q2497|WD=Q2497}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Ishikawa|label=Miwa Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Miwa Shrine''' (みわじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Aza Kitanakajō, {{ill|lt=Tsubata|Q432014|WD=Q432014}}, {{ill|lt=Kahoku District|Q1130942|WD=Q1130942}}, {{ill|lt=Ishikawa Prefecture|Q131281|WD=Q131281}}. It stands in the hills behind {{ill|lt=Tsubata Station|Q3701490|WD=Q3701490}}. Its {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=former shrine rank|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q712534}} was {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. === Enshrined deities === It enshrines eight deities: {{ill|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi|Q867534|WD=Q867534|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Kukurihime|Q11619891|WD=Q11619891|lt=Kukurihime-no-Kami}}, {{ill|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi|Q21391580|WD=Q21391580|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q2564508}}, {{ill|lt=Himegami|Q22070227|WD=Q22070227}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, and {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10935105|lt=Ōyamakui-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}. === Origin === Its founding is said to be {{ill|lt=836|Q24957|WD=Q24957}} ({{ill|lt=Jōwa (Japan)|Q1075605|WD=Q1075605|lt=Jōwa}} 3), and it is considered the minor shrine "Miwa Shrine" in Kaga District, {{ill|lt=Kaga Province|Q864166|WD=Q864166}}, in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}Another candidate {{ill|lt=ronsha|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904}} is {{ill|lt=Ishiura Shrine|Q11586806|WD=Q11586806}} in {{ill|lt=Kanazawa|Q191130|WD=Q191130}}.. There is a pond in the precincts called Ryūjin-no-Ike. === Access === * 10 minutes on foot from Tsubata Station on {{ill|lt=West Japan Railway Company|Q502125|WD=Q502125}} (JR West) and {{ill|lt=IR Ishikawa Railway|Q11585936|WD=Q11585936}} === Notes === {{reflist}} ===References=== * Ishikawa Prefectural Shrine Office (ed.), ''Ishikawa-ken Jinja-shi'', Ishikawa Prefectural Shrine Office, October 26, 1976 === External links === *[https://www.ishikawa-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/j0931/ 三輪神社] (Ishikawa Prefectural Shrine Office) {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:みわしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Miwa Shrine (Q11357406)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Miwa Shrine (Q11357406)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kaga Province]] [[Category:石川県の神社]] [[Category:加賀国の式内社]] [[Category:石川県の旧村社]] [[Category:津幡町の建築物]] [[Category:津幡町の歴史]] [[Category:三輪神社]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QMiwa Shrine (Q11357406)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QMiwa Shrine (Q11357406)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Miwa Shrine (Q11357406)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

(disambiguation)|UNKNOWN|lt=Miwa Shrine}}}} {{神社 |名称 = Miwa Shrine |画像 = [[画像:MIwajinja-tubata.jpg|250px]]
Torii |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Ishikawa Prefecture|Q131281|WD=Q131281|1=Ishikawa Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kahoku District|Q1130942|WD=Q1130942|1=Kahoku District, Ishikawa}}{{ill|lt=Tsubata|Q432014|WD=Q432014|1=Tsubata, Ishikawa}} Aza Kitanakajō Sa-no-2-no-2 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi|Q867534|WD=Q867534|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōmononushi}}, plus seven other deities |社格 = candidate for {{ill|lt=Shikinai Shōsha|Q134917287|WD=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}}, former {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = tradition: {{ill|lt=836|Q24957|WD=Q24957|1=836}} (Jōwa 3) |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=April 15|Q2497|WD=Q2497|1=April 15}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Ishikawa|label=Miwa Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Miwa Shrine''' (みわじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Aza Kitanakajō, {{ill|lt=Tsubata|Q432014|WD=Q432014|1=Tsubata, Ishikawa}}, {{ill|lt=Kahoku District|Q1130942|WD=Q1130942|1=Kahoku District, Ishikawa}}, {{ill|lt=Ishikawa Prefecture|Q131281|WD=Q131281|1=Ishikawa Prefecture}}. It stands in the hills behind {{ill|lt=Tsubata Station|Q3701490|WD=Q3701490|1=Tsubata Station}}. Its {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=former shrine rank|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534}} was {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === It enshrines eight deities: {{ill|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi|Q867534|WD=Q867534|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōmononushi}}, {{ill|lt=Kukurihime|Q11619891|WD=Q11619891|lt=Kukurihime-no-Kami|1=Kukurihime}}, {{ill|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi|Q21391580|WD=Q21391580|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Mikoto|1=Futsunushi}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Kami|1=Takemikazuchi}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane|WD=Q2564508}}, {{ill|lt=Himegami|Q22070227|WD=Q22070227|1=Himegami}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|1=Ukanomitama}}, and {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10935105|lt=Ōyamakui-no-Mikoto|1=Oyamakui no Kami}}. === Origin === Its founding is said to be {{ill|lt=836|Q24957|WD=Q24957|1=836}} ({{ill|lt=Jōwa (Japan)|Q1075605|WD=Q1075605|lt=Jōwa|1=Jōwa (Heian period)}} 3), and it is considered the minor shrine "Miwa Shrine" in Kaga District, {{ill|lt=Kaga Province|Q864166|WD=Q864166|1=Ise Province}}, in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}Another candidate {{ill|lt=ronsha|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} is {{ill|lt=Ishiura Shrine|Q11586806|WD=Q11586806|1=Ishiura Shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kanazawa|Q191130|WD=Q191130|1=Kanazawa}}.. There is a pond in the precincts called Ryūjin-no-Ike. === Access === * 10 minutes on foot from Tsubata Station on {{ill|lt=West Japan Railway Company|Q502125|WD=Q502125|1=West Japan Railway Company}} (JR West) and {{ill|lt=IR Ishikawa Railway|Q11585936|WD=Q11585936|1=IR Ishikawa Railway}} === Notes === {{reflist}} ===References=== * Ishikawa Prefectural Shrine Office (ed.), ''Ishikawa-ken Jinja-shi'', Ishikawa Prefectural Shrine Office, October 26, 1976 === External links === *[https://www.ishikawa-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/j0931/ 三輪神社] (Ishikawa Prefectural Shrine Office) {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:みわしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Miwa Shrine (Q11357406)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Miwa Shrine (Q11357406)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kaga Province]] [[Category:石川県の神社]] [[Category:加賀国の式内社]] [[Category:石川県の旧村社]] [[Category:津幡町の建築物]] [[Category:津幡町の歴史]] [[Category:三輪神社]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QMiwa Shrine (Q11357406)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QMiwa Shrine (Q11357406)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Miwa Shrine (Q11357406)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

Torii |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Ishikawa Prefecture|Q131281|WD=Q131281|1=Ishikawa Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kahoku District|Q1130942|WD=Q1130942|1=Kahoku District, Ishikawa}}{{ill|lt=Tsubata|Q432014|WD=Q432014|1=Tsubata, Ishikawa}} Aza Kitanakajō Sa-no-2-no-2 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi|Q867534|WD=Q867534|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōmononushi}}, plus seven other deities |社格 = candidate for {{ill|lt=Shikinai Shōsha|Q134917287|WD=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}}, former {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = tradition: {{ill|lt=836|Q24957|WD=Q24957|1=836}} (Jōwa 3) |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=April 15|Q2497|WD=Q2497|1=April 15}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Ishikawa|label=Miwa Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Miwa Shrine''' (みわじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Aza Kitanakajō, {{ill|lt=Tsubata|Q432014|WD=Q432014|1=Tsubata, Ishikawa}}, {{ill|lt=Kahoku District|Q1130942|WD=Q1130942|1=Kahoku District, Ishikawa}}, {{ill|lt=Ishikawa Prefecture|Q131281|WD=Q131281|1=Ishikawa Prefecture}}. It stands in the hills behind {{ill|lt=Tsubata Station|Q3701490|WD=Q3701490|1=Tsubata Station}}. Its {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=former shrine rank|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534}} was {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === It enshrines eight deities: {{ill|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi|Q867534|WD=Q867534|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōmononushi}}, {{ill|lt=Kukurihime|Q11619891|WD=Q11619891|lt=Kukurihime-no-Kami|1=Kukurihime}}, {{ill|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi|Q21391580|WD=Q21391580|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Mikoto|1=Futsunushi}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Kami|1=Takemikazuchi}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane|WD=Q2564508}}, {{ill|lt=Himegami|Q22070227|WD=Q22070227|1=Himegami}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|1=Ukanomitama}}, and {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10935105|lt=Ōyamakui-no-Mikoto|1=Oyamakui no Kami}}. === Origin === Its founding is said to be {{ill|lt=836|Q24957|WD=Q24957|1=836}} ({{ill|lt=Jōwa (Japan)|Q1075605|WD=Q1075605|lt=Jōwa|1=Jōwa (Heian period)}} 3), and it is considered the minor shrine "Miwa Shrine" in Kaga District, {{ill|lt=Kaga Province|Q864166|WD=Q864166|1=Ise Province}}, in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}Another candidate {{ill|lt=ronsha|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} is {{ill|lt=Ishiura Shrine|Q11586806|WD=Q11586806|1=Ishiura Shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kanazawa|Q191130|WD=Q191130|1=Kanazawa}}.. There is a pond in the precincts called Ryūjin-no-Ike. === Access === * 10 minutes on foot from Tsubata Station on {{ill|lt=West Japan Railway Company|Q502125|WD=Q502125|1=West Japan Railway Company}} (JR West) and {{ill|lt=IR Ishikawa Railway|Q11585936|WD=Q11585936|1=IR Ishikawa Railway}} === Notes === {{reflist}} ===References=== * Ishikawa Prefectural Shrine Office (ed.), ''Ishikawa-ken Jinja-shi'', Ishikawa Prefectural Shrine Office, October 26, 1976 === External links === *[https://www.ishikawa-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/j0931/ 三輪神社] (Ishikawa Prefectural Shrine Office) {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:みわしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Miwa Shrine (Q11357406)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Miwa Shrine (Q11357406)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kaga Province]] [[Category:石川県の神社]] [[Category:加賀国の式内社]] [[Category:石川県の旧村社]] [[Category:津幡町の建築物]] [[Category:津幡町の歴史]] [[Category:三輪神社]] {{DEFAULTSORT:QMiwa Shrine (Q11357406)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QMiwa Shrine (Q11357406)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Miwa Shrine (Q11357406)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

Torii |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Ishikawa Prefecture|Q131281|WD=Q131281|1=Ishikawa Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kahoku District|Q1130942|WD=Q1130942|1=Kahoku District, Ishikawa}}{{ill|lt=Tsubata|Q432014|WD=Q432014|1=Tsubata, Ishikawa}} Aza Kitanakajō Sa-no-2-no-2 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi|Q867534|WD=Q867534|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōmononushi}}, plus seven other deities |社格 = candidate for {{ill|lt=Shikinai Shōsha|Q134917287|WD=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}}, former {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = tradition: {{ill|lt=836|Q24957|WD=Q24957|1=836}} (Jōwa 3) |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=April 15|Q2497|WD=Q2497|1=April 15}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Ishikawa|label=Miwa Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Miwa Shrine''' (みわじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Aza Kitanakajō, {{ill|lt=Tsubata|Q432014|WD=Q432014|1=Tsubata, Ishikawa}}, {{ill|lt=Kahoku District|Q1130942|WD=Q1130942|1=Kahoku District, Ishikawa}}, {{ill|lt=Ishikawa Prefecture|Q131281|WD=Q131281|1=Ishikawa Prefecture}}. It stands in the hills behind {{ill|lt=Tsubata Station|Q3701490|WD=Q3701490|1=Tsubata Station}}. Its {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=former shrine rank|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534}} was {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === It enshrines eight deities: {{ill|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi|Q867534|WD=Q867534|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōmononushi}}, {{ill|lt=Kukurihime|Q11619891|WD=Q11619891|lt=Kukurihime-no-Kami|1=Kukurihime}}, {{ill|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi|Q21391580|WD=Q21391580|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Mikoto|1=Futsunushi}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Kami|1=Takemikazuchi}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane|WD=Q2564508}}, {{ill|lt=Himegami|Q22070227|WD=Q22070227|1=Himegami}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|1=Ukanomitama}}, and {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10935105|lt=Ōyamakui-no-Mikoto|1=Oyamakui no Kami}}. === Origin === Its founding is said to be {{ill|lt=836|Q24957|WD=Q24957|1=836}} ({{ill|lt=Jōwa (Japan)|Q1075605|WD=Q1075605|lt=Jōwa|1=Jōwa (Heian period)}} 3), and it is considered the minor shrine "Miwa Shrine" in Kaga District, {{ill|lt=Kaga Province|Q864166|WD=Q864166|1=Ise Province}}, in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}Another candidate {{ill|lt=ronsha|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} is {{ill|lt=Ishiura Shrine|Q11586806|WD=Q11586806|1=Ishiura Shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kanazawa|Q191130|WD=Q191130|1=Kanazawa}}.. There is a pond in the precincts called Ryūjin-no-Ike. === Access === * 10 minutes on foot from Tsubata Station on {{ill|lt=West Japan Railway Company|Q502125|WD=Q502125|1=West Japan Railway Company}} (JR West) and {{ill|lt=IR Ishikawa Railway|Q11585936|WD=Q11585936|1=IR Ishikawa Railway}} === Notes === {{reflist}} ===References=== * Ishikawa Prefectural Shrine Office (ed.), ''Ishikawa-ken Jinja-shi'', Ishikawa Prefectural Shrine Office, October 26, 1976 === External links === *[https://www.ishikawa-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/j0931/ 三輪神社] (Ishikawa Prefectural Shrine Office) {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:みわしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Miwa Shrine (Q11357406)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Miwa Shrine (Q11357406)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kaga Province]] [[Category:石川県の神社]] [[Category:加賀国の式内社]] [[Category:石川県の旧村社]] [[Category:津幡町の建築物]] [[Category:津幡町の歴史]] [[Category:三輪神社]] {{DEFAULTSORT:QMiwa Shrine (Q11357406)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QMiwa Shrine (Q11357406)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Miwa Shrine (Q11357406)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

(津幡町)]] {{wikidata link|Q11357406}} == Japanese content == {{otheruses|Miwa Shrine in Tsubata, Kahoku District, Ishikawa Prefecture|other Miwa Shrines|{{ill|lt=Miwa Shrine (disambiguation)|UNKNOWN|lt=Miwa Shrine}}}} {{神社 |名称 = Miwa Shrine |画像 = [[画像:MIwajinja-tubata.jpg|250px]]
Torii |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Ishikawa Prefecture|Q131281|WD=Q131281|1=Ishikawa Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kahoku District|Q1130942|WD=Q1130942|1=Kahoku District, Ishikawa}}{{ill|lt=Tsubata|Q432014|WD=Q432014|1=Tsubata, Ishikawa}} Aza Kitanakajō Sa-no-2-no-2 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi|Q867534|WD=Q867534|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōmononushi}}, plus seven other deities |社格 = candidate for {{ill|lt=Shikinai Shōsha|Q134917287|WD=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}}, former {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = tradition: {{ill|lt=836|Q24957|WD=Q24957|1=836}} (Jōwa 3) |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=April 15|Q2497|WD=Q2497|1=April 15}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Ishikawa|label=Miwa Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Miwa Shrine''' (みわじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Aza Kitanakajō, {{ill|lt=Tsubata|Q432014|WD=Q432014|1=Tsubata, Ishikawa}}, {{ill|lt=Kahoku District|Q1130942|WD=Q1130942|1=Kahoku District, Ishikawa}}, {{ill|lt=Ishikawa Prefecture|Q131281|WD=Q131281|1=Ishikawa Prefecture}}. It stands in the hills behind {{ill|lt=Tsubata Station|Q3701490|WD=Q3701490|1=Tsubata Station}}. Its {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=former shrine rank|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534}} was {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === It enshrines eight deities: {{ill|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi|Q867534|WD=Q867534|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōmononushi}}, {{ill|lt=Kukurihime|Q11619891|WD=Q11619891|lt=Kukurihime-no-Kami|1=Kukurihime}}, {{ill|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi|Q21391580|WD=Q21391580|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Mikoto|1=Futsunushi}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Kami|1=Takemikazuchi}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane|WD=Q2564508}}, {{ill|lt=Himegami|Q22070227|WD=Q22070227|1=Himegami}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|1=Ukanomitama}}, and {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10935105|lt=Ōyamakui-no-Mikoto|1=Oyamakui no Kami}}. === Origin === Its founding is said to be {{ill|lt=836|Q24957|WD=Q24957|1=836}} ({{ill|lt=Jōwa (Japan)|Q1075605|WD=Q1075605|lt=Jōwa|1=Jōwa (Heian period)}} 3), and it is considered the minor shrine "Miwa Shrine" in Kaga District, {{ill|lt=Kaga Province|Q864166|WD=Q864166|1=Ise Province}}, in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}Another candidate {{ill|lt=ronsha|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} is {{ill|lt=Ishiura Shrine|Q11586806|WD=Q11586806|1=Ishiura Shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kanazawa|Q191130|WD=Q191130|1=Kanazawa}}.. There is a pond in the precincts called Ryūjin-no-Ike. === Access === * 10 minutes on foot from Tsubata Station on {{ill|lt=West Japan Railway Company|Q502125|WD=Q502125|1=West Japan Railway Company}} (JR West) and {{ill|lt=IR Ishikawa Railway|Q11585936|WD=Q11585936|1=IR Ishikawa Railway}} === Notes === {{reflist}} ===References=== * Ishikawa Prefectural Shrine Office (ed.), ''Ishikawa-ken Jinja-shi'', Ishikawa Prefectural Shrine Office, October 26, 1976 === External links === *[https://www.ishikawa-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/j0931/ 三輪神社] (Ishikawa Prefectural Shrine Office) {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:みわしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Miwa Shrine (Q11357406)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Miwa Shrine (Q11357406)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kaga Province]] [[Category:石川県の神社]] [[Category:加賀国の式内社]] [[Category:石川県の旧村社]] [[Category:津幡町の建築物]] [[Category:津幡町の歴史]] [[Category:三輪神社]] {{DEFAULTSORT:QMiwa Shrine (Q11357406)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QMiwa Shrine (Q11357406)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Miwa Shrine (Q11357406)}}[[Category:qq]]

(津幡町)]] {{wikidata link|Q11357406}} == Japanese content == {{otheruses|Miwa Shrine in Tsubata, Kahoku District, Ishikawa Prefecture|other Miwa Shrines|{{ill|lt=Miwa Shrine (disambiguation)|UNKNOWN|lt=Miwa Shrine}}}} {{神社 |名称 = Miwa Shrine |画像 = [[画像:MIwajinja-tubata.jpg|250px]]
Torii |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Ishikawa Prefecture|Q131281|WD=Q131281|1=Ishikawa Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kahoku District|Q1130942|WD=Q1130942|1=Kahoku District, Ishikawa}}{{ill|lt=Tsubata|Q432014|WD=Q432014|1=Tsubata, Ishikawa}} Aza Kitanakajō Sa-no-2-no-2 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi|Q867534|WD=Q867534|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōmononushi}}, plus seven other deities |社格 = candidate for {{ill|lt=Shikinai Shōsha|Q134917287|WD=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}}, former {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = tradition: {{ill|lt=836|Q24957|WD=Q24957|1=836}} (Jōwa 3) |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=April 15|Q2497|WD=Q2497|1=April 15}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Ishikawa|label=Miwa Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Miwa Shrine''' (みわじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Aza Kitanakajō, {{ill|lt=Tsubata|Q432014|WD=Q432014|1=Tsubata, Ishikawa}}, {{ill|lt=Kahoku District|Q1130942|WD=Q1130942|1=Kahoku District, Ishikawa}}, {{ill|lt=Ishikawa Prefecture|Q131281|WD=Q131281|1=Ishikawa Prefecture}}. It stands in the hills behind {{ill|lt=Tsubata Station|Q3701490|WD=Q3701490|1=Tsubata Station}}. Its {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=former shrine rank|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534}} was {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === It enshrines eight deities: {{ill|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi|Q867534|WD=Q867534|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōmononushi}}, {{ill|lt=Kukurihime|Q11619891|WD=Q11619891|lt=Kukurihime-no-Kami|1=Kukurihime}}, {{ill|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi|Q21391580|WD=Q21391580|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Mikoto|1=Futsunushi}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Kami|1=Takemikazuchi}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane|WD=Q2564508}}, {{ill|lt=Himegami|Q22070227|WD=Q22070227|1=Himegami}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|1=Ukanomitama}}, and {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10935105|lt=Ōyamakui-no-Mikoto|1=Oyamakui no Kami}}. === Origin === Its founding is said to be {{ill|lt=836|Q24957|WD=Q24957|1=836}} ({{ill|lt=Jōwa (Japan)|Q1075605|WD=Q1075605|lt=Jōwa|1=Jōwa (Heian period)}} 3), and it is considered the minor shrine "Miwa Shrine" in Kaga District, {{ill|lt=Kaga Province|Q864166|WD=Q864166|1=Ise Province}}, in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}Another candidate {{ill|lt=ronsha|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} is {{ill|lt=Ishiura Shrine|Q11586806|WD=Q11586806|1=Ishiura Shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kanazawa|Q191130|WD=Q191130|1=Kanazawa}}.. There is a pond in the precincts called Ryūjin-no-Ike. === Access === * 10 minutes on foot from Tsubata Station on {{ill|lt=West Japan Railway Company|Q502125|WD=Q502125|1=West Japan Railway Company}} (JR West) and {{ill|lt=IR Ishikawa Railway|Q11585936|WD=Q11585936|1=IR Ishikawa Railway}} === Notes === {{reflist}} ===References=== * Ishikawa Prefectural Shrine Office (ed.), ''Ishikawa-ken Jinja-shi'', Ishikawa Prefectural Shrine Office, October 26, 1976 === External links === *[https://www.ishikawa-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/j0931/ 三輪神社] (Ishikawa Prefectural Shrine Office) {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Miwa Shrine (Q11357406)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Miwa Shrine (Q11357406)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kaga Province]] [[Category:石川県の神社]] [[Category:加賀国の式内社]] [[Category:石川県の旧村社]] [[Category:津幡町の建築物]] [[Category:津幡町の歴史]] [[Category:三輪神社]] [[Category:qq]]

(津幡町)]] {{wikidata link|Q11357406}} == Japanese content == {{otheruses|Miwa Shrine in Tsubata, Kahoku District, Ishikawa Prefecture|other Miwa Shrines|{{ill|lt=Miwa Shrine (disambiguation)|UNKNOWN|lt=Miwa Shrine}}}} {{神社 |名称 = Miwa Shrine |画像 = [[画像:MIwajinja-tubata.jpg|250px]]
Torii |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Ishikawa Prefecture|Q131281|WD=Q131281|1=Ishikawa Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kahoku District|Q1130942|WD=Q1130942|1=Kahoku District, Ishikawa}}{{ill|lt=Tsubata|Q432014|WD=Q432014|1=Tsubata, Ishikawa}} Aza Kitanakajō Sa-no-2-no-2 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi|Q867534|WD=Q867534|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōmononushi}}, plus seven other deities |社格 = candidate for {{ill|lt=Shikinai Shōsha|Q134917287|WD=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}}, former {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = tradition: {{ill|lt=836|Q24957|WD=Q24957|1=836}} (Jōwa 3) |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=April 15|Q2497|WD=Q2497|1=April 15}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Ishikawa|label=Miwa Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Miwa Shrine''' (みわじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Aza Kitanakajō, {{ill|lt=Tsubata|Q432014|WD=Q432014|1=Tsubata, Ishikawa}}, {{ill|lt=Kahoku District|Q1130942|WD=Q1130942|1=Kahoku District, Ishikawa}}, {{ill|lt=Ishikawa Prefecture|Q131281|WD=Q131281|1=Ishikawa Prefecture}}. It stands in the hills behind {{ill|lt=Tsubata Station|Q3701490|WD=Q3701490|1=Tsubata Station}}. Its {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=former shrine rank|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534}} was {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === It enshrines eight deities: {{ill|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi|Q867534|WD=Q867534|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōmononushi}}, {{ill|lt=Kukurihime|Q11619891|WD=Q11619891|lt=Kukurihime-no-Kami|1=Kukurihime}}, {{ill|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi|Q21391580|WD=Q21391580|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Mikoto|1=Futsunushi}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Kami|1=Takemikazuchi}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane|WD=Q2564508}}, {{ill|lt=Himegami|Q22070227|WD=Q22070227|1=Himegami}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|1=Ukanomitama}}, and {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10935105|lt=Ōyamakui-no-Mikoto|1=Oyamakui no Kami}}. === Origin === Its founding is said to be {{ill|lt=836|Q24957|WD=Q24957|1=836}} ({{ill|lt=Jōwa (Japan)|Q1075605|WD=Q1075605|lt=Jōwa|1=Jōwa (Heian period)}} 3), and it is considered the minor shrine "Miwa Shrine" in Kaga District, {{ill|lt=Kaga Province|Q864166|WD=Q864166|1=Ise Province}}, in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}Another candidate {{ill|lt=ronsha|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} is {{ill|lt=Ishiura Shrine|Q11586806|WD=Q11586806|1=Ishiura Shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kanazawa|Q191130|WD=Q191130|1=Kanazawa}}.. There is a pond in the precincts called Ryūjin-no-Ike. === Access === * 10 minutes on foot from Tsubata Station on {{ill|lt=West Japan Railway Company|Q502125|WD=Q502125|1=West Japan Railway Company}} (JR West) and {{ill|lt=IR Ishikawa Railway|Q11585936|WD=Q11585936|1=IR Ishikawa Railway}} === Notes === {{reflist}} ===References=== * Ishikawa Prefectural Shrine Office (ed.), ''Ishikawa-ken Jinja-shi'', Ishikawa Prefectural Shrine Office, October 26, 1976 === External links === *[https://www.ishikawa-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/j0931/ 三輪神社] (Ishikawa Prefectural Shrine Office) {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Miwa Shrine (Q11357406)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Miwa Shrine (Q11357406)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kaga Province]] [[Category:石川県の神社]] [[Category:加賀国の式内社]] [[Category:石川県の旧村社]] [[Category:津幡町の建築物]] [[Category:津幡町の歴史]] [[Category:Miwa shrines]] [[Category:qq]]

(津幡町)]] {{wikidata link|Q11357406}} == Japanese content == {{otheruses|Miwa Shrine in Tsubata, Kahoku District, Ishikawa Prefecture|other Miwa Shrines|{{ill|lt=Miwa Shrine (disambiguation)|UNKNOWN|lt=Miwa Shrine}}}} {{神社 |名称 = Miwa Shrine |画像 = [[画像:MIwajinja-tubata.jpg|250px]]
Torii |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Ishikawa Prefecture|Q131281|WD=Q131281|1=Ishikawa Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kahoku District|Q1130942|WD=Q1130942|1=Kahoku District, Ishikawa}}{{ill|lt=Tsubata|Q432014|WD=Q432014|1=Tsubata, Ishikawa}} Aza Kitanakajō Sa-no-2-no-2 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi|Q867534|WD=Q867534|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōmononushi}}, plus seven other deities |社格 = candidate for {{ill|lt=Shikinai Shōsha|Q134917287|WD=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}}, former {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = tradition: {{ill|lt=836|Q24957|WD=Q24957|1=836}} (Jōwa 3) |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=April 15|Q2497|WD=Q2497|1=April 15}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Ishikawa|label=Miwa Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Miwa Shrine''' (みわじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Aza Kitanakajō, {{ill|lt=Tsubata|Q432014|WD=Q432014|1=Tsubata, Ishikawa}}, {{ill|lt=Kahoku District|Q1130942|WD=Q1130942|1=Kahoku District, Ishikawa}}, {{ill|lt=Ishikawa Prefecture|Q131281|WD=Q131281|1=Ishikawa Prefecture}}. It stands in the hills behind {{ill|lt=Tsubata Station|Q3701490|WD=Q3701490|1=Tsubata Station}}. Its {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=former shrine rank|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534}} was {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === It enshrines eight deities: {{ill|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi|Q867534|WD=Q867534|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōmononushi}}, {{ill|lt=Kukurihime|Q11619891|WD=Q11619891|lt=Kukurihime-no-Kami|1=Kukurihime}}, {{ill|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi|Q21391580|WD=Q21391580|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Mikoto|1=Futsunushi}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Kami|1=Takemikazuchi}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane|WD=Q2564508}}, {{ill|lt=Himegami|Q22070227|WD=Q22070227|1=Himegami}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|1=Ukanomitama}}, and {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10935105|lt=Ōyamakui-no-Mikoto|1=Oyamakui no Kami}}. === Origin === Its founding is said to be {{ill|lt=836|Q24957|WD=Q24957|1=836}} ({{ill|lt=Jōwa (Japan)|Q1075605|WD=Q1075605|lt=Jōwa|1=Jōwa (Heian period)}} 3), and it is considered the minor shrine "Miwa Shrine" in Kaga District, {{ill|lt=Kaga Province|Q864166|WD=Q864166|1=Ise Province}}, in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}Another candidate {{ill|lt=ronsha|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} is {{ill|lt=Ishiura Shrine|Q11586806|WD=Q11586806|1=Ishiura Shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kanazawa|Q191130|WD=Q191130|1=Kanazawa}}.. There is a pond in the precincts called Ryūjin-no-Ike. === Access === * 10 minutes on foot from Tsubata Station on {{ill|lt=West Japan Railway Company|Q502125|WD=Q502125|1=West Japan Railway Company}} (JR West) and {{ill|lt=IR Ishikawa Railway|Q11585936|WD=Q11585936|1=IR Ishikawa Railway}} === Notes === {{reflist}} ===References=== * Ishikawa Prefectural Shrine Office (ed.), ''Ishikawa-ken Jinja-shi'', Ishikawa Prefectural Shrine Office, October 26, 1976 === External links === *[https://www.ishikawa-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/j0931/ 三輪神社] (Ishikawa Prefectural Shrine Office) {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Miwa Shrine (Q11357406)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Miwa Shrine (Q11357406)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kaga Province]] [[Category:石川県の神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kaga Province]] [[Category:石川県の旧村社]] [[Category:津幡町の建築物]] [[Category:津幡町の歴史]] [[Category:Miwa shrines]] [[Category:qq]]

(津幡町)]] {{wikidata link|Q11357406}} == Japanese content == {{otheruses|Miwa Shrine in Tsubata, Kahoku District, Ishikawa Prefecture|other Miwa Shrines|{{ill|lt=Miwa Shrine (disambiguation)|UNKNOWN|lt=Miwa Shrine}}}} {{神社 |名称 = Miwa Shrine |画像 = [[画像:MIwajinja-tubata.jpg|250px]]
Torii |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Ishikawa Prefecture|Q131281|WD=Q131281|1=Ishikawa Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kahoku District|Q1130942|WD=Q1130942|1=Kahoku District, Ishikawa}}{{ill|lt=Tsubata|Q432014|WD=Q432014|1=Tsubata, Ishikawa}} Aza Kitanakajō Sa-no-2-no-2 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi|Q867534|WD=Q867534|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōmononushi}}, plus seven other deities |社格 = candidate for {{ill|lt=Shikinai Shōsha|Q134917287|WD=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}}, former {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = tradition: {{ill|lt=836|Q24957|WD=Q24957|1=836}} (Jōwa 3) |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=April 15|Q2497|WD=Q2497|1=April 15}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Ishikawa|label=Miwa Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Miwa Shrine''' (みわじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Aza Kitanakajō, {{ill|lt=Tsubata|Q432014|WD=Q432014|1=Tsubata, Ishikawa}}, {{ill|lt=Kahoku District|Q1130942|WD=Q1130942|1=Kahoku District, Ishikawa}}, {{ill|lt=Ishikawa Prefecture|Q131281|WD=Q131281|1=Ishikawa Prefecture}}. It stands in the hills behind {{ill|lt=Tsubata Station|Q3701490|WD=Q3701490|1=Tsubata Station}}. Its {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=former shrine rank|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534}} was {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === It enshrines eight deities: {{ill|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi|Q867534|WD=Q867534|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōmononushi}}, {{ill|lt=Kukurihime|Q11619891|WD=Q11619891|lt=Kukurihime-no-Kami|1=Kukurihime}}, {{ill|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi|Q21391580|WD=Q21391580|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Mikoto|1=Futsunushi}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Kami|1=Takemikazuchi}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane|WD=Q2564508}}, {{ill|lt=Himegami|Q22070227|WD=Q22070227|1=Himegami}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|1=Ukanomitama}}, and {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10935105|lt=Ōyamakui-no-Mikoto|1=Oyamakui no Kami}}. === Origin === Its founding is said to be {{ill|lt=836|Q24957|WD=Q24957|1=836}} ({{ill|lt=Jōwa (Japan)|Q1075605|WD=Q1075605|lt=Jōwa|1=Jōwa (Heian period)}} 3), and it is considered the minor shrine "Miwa Shrine" in Kaga District, {{ill|lt=Kaga Province|Q864166|WD=Q864166|1=Ise Province}}, in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}Another candidate {{ill|lt=ronsha|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} is {{ill|lt=Ishiura Shrine|Q11586806|WD=Q11586806|1=Ishiura Shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kanazawa|Q191130|WD=Q191130|1=Kanazawa}}.. There is a pond in the precincts called Ryūjin-no-Ike. === Access === * 10 minutes on foot from Tsubata Station on {{ill|lt=West Japan Railway Company|Q502125|WD=Q502125|1=West Japan Railway Company}} (JR West) and {{ill|lt=IR Ishikawa Railway|Q11585936|WD=Q11585936|1=IR Ishikawa Railway}} === Notes === {{reflist}} ===References=== * Ishikawa Prefectural Shrine Office (ed.), ''Ishikawa-ken Jinja-shi'', Ishikawa Prefectural Shrine Office, October 26, 1976 === External links === *[https://www.ishikawa-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/j0931/ 三輪神社] (Ishikawa Prefectural Shrine Office) {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Miwa Shrine (Q11357406)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Miwa Shrine (Q11357406)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kaga Province]] [[Category:石川県の神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kaga Province]] [[Category:石川県の旧村社]] [[Category:Buildings in Tsubata Town]] [[Category:津幡町の歴史]] [[Category:Miwa shrines]] [[Category:qq]]

(津幡町)]] {{wikidata link|Q11357406}} == Japanese content == {{otheruses|Miwa Shrine in Tsubata, Kahoku District, Ishikawa Prefecture|other Miwa Shrines|{{ill|lt=Miwa Shrine (disambiguation)|UNKNOWN|lt=Miwa Shrine}}}} {{神社 |名称 = Miwa Shrine |画像 = [[画像:MIwajinja-tubata.jpg|250px]]
Torii |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Ishikawa Prefecture|Q131281|WD=Q131281|1=Ishikawa Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kahoku District|Q1130942|WD=Q1130942|1=Kahoku District, Ishikawa}}{{ill|lt=Tsubata|Q432014|WD=Q432014|1=Tsubata, Ishikawa}} Aza Kitanakajō Sa-no-2-no-2 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi|Q867534|WD=Q867534|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōmononushi}}, plus seven other deities |社格 = candidate for {{ill|lt=Shikinai Shōsha|Q134917287|WD=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}}, former {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = tradition: {{ill|lt=836|Q24957|WD=Q24957|1=836}} (Jōwa 3) |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=April 15|Q2497|WD=Q2497|1=April 15}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Ishikawa|label=Miwa Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Miwa Shrine''' (みわじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Aza Kitanakajō, {{ill|lt=Tsubata|Q432014|WD=Q432014|1=Tsubata, Ishikawa}}, {{ill|lt=Kahoku District|Q1130942|WD=Q1130942|1=Kahoku District, Ishikawa}}, {{ill|lt=Ishikawa Prefecture|Q131281|WD=Q131281|1=Ishikawa Prefecture}}. It stands in the hills behind {{ill|lt=Tsubata Station|Q3701490|WD=Q3701490|1=Tsubata Station}}. Its {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=former shrine rank|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534}} was {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === It enshrines eight deities: {{ill|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi|Q867534|WD=Q867534|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōmononushi}}, {{ill|lt=Kukurihime|Q11619891|WD=Q11619891|lt=Kukurihime-no-Kami|1=Kukurihime}}, {{ill|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi|Q21391580|WD=Q21391580|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Mikoto|1=Futsunushi}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Kami|1=Takemikazuchi}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane|WD=Q2564508}}, {{ill|lt=Himegami|Q22070227|WD=Q22070227|1=Himegami}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|1=Ukanomitama}}, and {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10935105|lt=Ōyamakui-no-Mikoto|1=Oyamakui no Kami}}. === Origin === Its founding is said to be {{ill|lt=836|Q24957|WD=Q24957|1=836}} ({{ill|lt=Jōwa (Japan)|Q1075605|WD=Q1075605|lt=Jōwa|1=Jōwa (Heian period)}} 3), and it is considered the minor shrine "Miwa Shrine" in Kaga District, {{ill|lt=Kaga Province|Q864166|WD=Q864166|1=Ise Province}}, in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}Another candidate {{ill|lt=ronsha|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} is {{ill|lt=Ishiura Shrine|Q11586806|WD=Q11586806|1=Ishiura Shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kanazawa|Q191130|WD=Q191130|1=Kanazawa}}.. There is a pond in the precincts called Ryūjin-no-Ike. === Access === * 10 minutes on foot from Tsubata Station on {{ill|lt=West Japan Railway Company|Q502125|WD=Q502125|1=West Japan Railway Company}} (JR West) and {{ill|lt=IR Ishikawa Railway|Q11585936|WD=Q11585936|1=IR Ishikawa Railway}} === Notes === {{reflist}} ===References=== * Ishikawa Prefectural Shrine Office (ed.), ''Ishikawa-ken Jinja-shi'', Ishikawa Prefectural Shrine Office, October 26, 1976 === External links === *[https://www.ishikawa-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/j0931/ 三輪神社] (Ishikawa Prefectural Shrine Office) {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Miwa Shrine (Q11357406)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Miwa Shrine (Q11357406)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kaga Province]] [[Category:石川県の神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kaga Province]] [[Category:石川県の旧村社]] [[Category:Buildings in Tsubata Town]] [[Category:Municipal History of Tsubata]] [[Category:Miwa shrines]] [[Category:qq]]

Torii |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Ishikawa Prefecture|Q131281|WD=Q131281|1=Ishikawa Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kahoku District|Q1130942|WD=Q1130942|1=Kahoku District, Ishikawa}}{{ill|lt=Tsubata|Q432014|WD=Q432014|1=Tsubata, Ishikawa}} Aza Kitanakajō Sa-no-2-no-2 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi|Q867534|WD=Q867534|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōmononushi}}, plus seven other deities |社格 = candidate for {{ill|lt=Shikinai Shōsha|Q134917287|WD=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}}, former {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = tradition: {{ill|lt=836|Q24957|WD=Q24957|1=836}} (Jōwa 3) |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=April 15|Q2497|WD=Q2497|1=April 15}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Ishikawa|label=Miwa Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Miwa Shrine''' (みわじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Aza Kitanakajō, {{ill|lt=Tsubata|Q432014|WD=Q432014|1=Tsubata, Ishikawa}}, {{ill|lt=Kahoku District|Q1130942|WD=Q1130942|1=Kahoku District, Ishikawa}}, {{ill|lt=Ishikawa Prefecture|Q131281|WD=Q131281|1=Ishikawa Prefecture}}. It stands in the hills behind {{ill|lt=Tsubata Station|Q3701490|WD=Q3701490|1=Tsubata Station}}. Its {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=former shrine rank|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534}} was {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === It enshrines eight deities: {{ill|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi|Q867534|WD=Q867534|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōmononushi}}, {{ill|lt=Kukurihime|Q11619891|WD=Q11619891|lt=Kukurihime-no-Kami|1=Kukurihime}}, {{ill|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi|Q21391580|WD=Q21391580|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Mikoto|1=Futsunushi}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Kami|1=Takemikazuchi}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane|WD=Q2564508}}, {{ill|lt=Himegami|Q22070227|WD=Q22070227|1=Himegami}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|1=Ukanomitama}}, and {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10935105|lt=Ōyamakui-no-Mikoto|1=Oyamakui no Kami}}. === Origin === Its founding is said to be {{ill|lt=836|Q24957|WD=Q24957|1=836}} ({{ill|lt=Jōwa (Japan)|Q1075605|WD=Q1075605|lt=Jōwa|1=Jōwa (Heian period)}} 3), and it is considered the minor shrine "Miwa Shrine" in Kaga District, {{ill|lt=Kaga Province|Q864166|WD=Q864166|1=Ise Province}}, in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}Another candidate {{ill|lt=ronsha|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} is {{ill|lt=Ishiura Shrine|Q11586806|WD=Q11586806|1=Ishiura Shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kanazawa|Q191130|WD=Q191130|1=Kanazawa}}.. There is a pond in the precincts called Ryūjin-no-Ike. === Access === * 10 minutes on foot from Tsubata Station on {{ill|lt=West Japan Railway Company|Q502125|WD=Q502125|1=West Japan Railway Company}} (JR West) and {{ill|lt=IR Ishikawa Railway|Q11585936|WD=Q11585936|1=IR Ishikawa Railway}} === Notes === {{reflist}} ===References=== * Ishikawa Prefectural Shrine Office (ed.), ''Ishikawa-ken Jinja-shi'', Ishikawa Prefectural Shrine Office, October 26, 1976 === External links === *[https://www.ishikawa-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/j0931/ 三輪神社] (Ishikawa Prefectural Shrine Office) {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Miwa Shrine (Q11357406)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Miwa Shrine (Q11357406)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kaga Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ishikawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kaga Province]] [[Category:石川県の旧村社]] [[Category:Buildings in Tsubata Town]] [[Category:Municipal History of Tsubata]] [[Category:Miwa shrines]] [[Category:qq]]

Torii |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Ishikawa Prefecture|Q131281|WD=Q131281|1=Ishikawa Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kahoku District|Q1130942|WD=Q1130942|1=Kahoku District, Ishikawa}}{{ill|lt=Tsubata|Q432014|WD=Q432014|1=Tsubata, Ishikawa}} Aza Kitanakajō Sa-no-2-no-2 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi|Q867534|WD=Q867534|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōmononushi}}, plus seven other deities |社格 = candidate for {{ill|lt=Shikinai Shōsha|Q134917287|WD=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}}, former {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = tradition: {{ill|lt=836|Q24957|WD=Q24957|1=836}} (Jōwa 3) |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=April 15|Q2497|WD=Q2497|1=April 15}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Ishikawa|label=Miwa Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Miwa Shrine''' (みわじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Aza Kitanakajō, {{ill|lt=Tsubata|Q432014|WD=Q432014|1=Tsubata, Ishikawa}}, {{ill|lt=Kahoku District|Q1130942|WD=Q1130942|1=Kahoku District, Ishikawa}}, {{ill|lt=Ishikawa Prefecture|Q131281|WD=Q131281|1=Ishikawa Prefecture}}. It stands in the hills behind {{ill|lt=Tsubata Station|Q3701490|WD=Q3701490|1=Tsubata Station}}. Its {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=former shrine rank|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534}} was {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === It enshrines eight deities: {{ill|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi|Q867534|WD=Q867534|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōmononushi}}, {{ill|lt=Kukurihime|Q11619891|WD=Q11619891|lt=Kukurihime-no-Kami|1=Kukurihime}}, {{ill|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi|Q21391580|WD=Q21391580|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Mikoto|1=Futsunushi}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Kami|1=Takemikazuchi}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane|WD=Q2564508}}, {{ill|lt=Himegami|Q22070227|WD=Q22070227|1=Himegami}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|1=Ukanomitama}}, and {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10935105|lt=Ōyamakui-no-Mikoto|1=Oyamakui no Kami}}. === Origin === Its founding is said to be {{ill|lt=836|Q24957|WD=Q24957|1=836}} ({{ill|lt=Jōwa (Japan)|Q1075605|WD=Q1075605|lt=Jōwa|1=Jōwa (Heian period)}} 3), and it is considered the minor shrine "Miwa Shrine" in Kaga District, {{ill|lt=Kaga Province|Q864166|WD=Q864166|1=Ise Province}}, in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}Another candidate {{ill|lt=ronsha|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} is {{ill|lt=Ishiura Shrine|Q11586806|WD=Q11586806|1=Ishiura Shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kanazawa|Q191130|WD=Q191130|1=Kanazawa}}.. There is a pond in the precincts called Ryūjin-no-Ike. === Access === * 10 minutes on foot from Tsubata Station on {{ill|lt=West Japan Railway Company|Q502125|WD=Q502125|1=West Japan Railway Company}} (JR West) and {{ill|lt=IR Ishikawa Railway|Q11585936|WD=Q11585936|1=IR Ishikawa Railway}} === Notes === {{reflist}} ===References=== * Ishikawa Prefectural Shrine Office (ed.), ''Ishikawa-ken Jinja-shi'', Ishikawa Prefectural Shrine Office, October 26, 1976 === External links === *[https://www.ishikawa-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/j0931/ 三輪神社] (Ishikawa Prefectural Shrine Office) {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Miwa Shrine (Q11357406)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Miwa Shrine (Q11357406)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kaga Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ishikawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kaga Province]] [[Category:石川県の旧村社]] [[Category:Buildings in Tsubata Town]] [[Category:Municipal History of Tsubata]] [[Category:Miwa shrines]]

(津幡町)]] {{wikidata link|Q11357406}} == Japanese content == {{otheruses|Miwa Shrine in Tsubata, Kahoku District, Ishikawa Prefecture|other Miwa Shrines|{{ill|lt=Miwa Shrine (disambiguation)|UNKNOWN|lt=Miwa Shrine}}}} {{神社 |名称 = Miwa Shrine |画像 = [[画像:MIwajinja-tubata.jpg|250px]]
Torii |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Ishikawa Prefecture|Q131281|WD=Q131281|1=Ishikawa Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kahoku District|Q1130942|WD=Q1130942|1=Kahoku District, Ishikawa}}{{ill|lt=Tsubata|Q432014|WD=Q432014|1=Tsubata, Ishikawa}} Aza Kitanakajō Sa-no-2-no-2 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi|Q867534|WD=Q867534|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōmononushi}}, plus seven other deities |社格 = candidate for {{ill|lt=Shikinai Shōsha|Q134917287|WD=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}}, former {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = tradition: {{ill|lt=836|Q24957|WD=Q24957|1=836}} (Jōwa 3) |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=April 15|Q2497|WD=Q2497|1=April 15}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Ishikawa|label=Miwa Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Miwa Shrine''' (みわじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Aza Kitanakajō, {{ill|lt=Tsubata|Q432014|WD=Q432014|1=Tsubata, Ishikawa}}, {{ill|lt=Kahoku District|Q1130942|WD=Q1130942|1=Kahoku District, Ishikawa}}, {{ill|lt=Ishikawa Prefecture|Q131281|WD=Q131281|1=Ishikawa Prefecture}}. It stands in the hills behind {{ill|lt=Tsubata Station|Q3701490|WD=Q3701490|1=Tsubata Station}}. Its {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=former shrine rank|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534}} was {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === It enshrines eight deities: {{ill|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi|Q867534|WD=Q867534|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōmononushi}}, {{ill|lt=Kukurihime|Q11619891|WD=Q11619891|lt=Kukurihime-no-Kami|1=Kukurihime}}, {{ill|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi|Q21391580|WD=Q21391580|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Mikoto|1=Futsunushi}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Kami|1=Takemikazuchi}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane|WD=Q2564508}}, {{ill|lt=Himegami|Q22070227|WD=Q22070227|1=Himegami}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|1=Ukanomitama}}, and {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10935105|lt=Ōyamakui-no-Mikoto|1=Oyamakui no Kami}}. === Origin === Its founding is said to be {{ill|lt=836|Q24957|WD=Q24957|1=836}} ({{ill|lt=Jōwa (Japan)|Q1075605|WD=Q1075605|lt=Jōwa|1=Jōwa (Heian period)}} 3), and it is considered the minor shrine "Miwa Shrine" in Kaga District, {{ill|lt=Kaga Province|Q864166|WD=Q864166|1=Ise Province}}, in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}Another candidate {{ill|lt=ronsha|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} is {{ill|lt=Ishiura Shrine|Q11586806|WD=Q11586806|1=Ishiura Shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kanazawa|Q191130|WD=Q191130|1=Kanazawa}}.. There is a pond in the precincts called Ryūjin-no-Ike. === Access === * 10 minutes on foot from Tsubata Station on {{ill|lt=West Japan Railway Company|Q502125|WD=Q502125|1=West Japan Railway Company}} (JR West) and {{ill|lt=IR Ishikawa Railway|Q11585936|WD=Q11585936|1=IR Ishikawa Railway}} === Notes === {{reflist}} ===References=== * Ishikawa Prefectural Shrine Office (ed.), ''Ishikawa-ken Jinja-shi'', Ishikawa Prefectural Shrine Office, October 26, 1976 === External links === *[https://www.ishikawa-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/j0931/ 三輪神社] (Ishikawa Prefectural Shrine Office) {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Miwa Shrine (Q11357406)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Miwa Shrine (Q11357406)]][[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kaga Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ishikawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kaga Province]] [[Category:石川県の旧村社]] [[Category:Buildings in Tsubata Town]] [[Category:Municipal History of Tsubata]] [[Category:Miwa shrines]]

Torii |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Ishikawa Prefecture|Q131281|WD=Q131281|1=Ishikawa Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kahoku District|Q1130942|WD=Q1130942|1=Kahoku District, Ishikawa}}{{ill|lt=Tsubata|Q432014|WD=Q432014|1=Tsubata, Ishikawa}} Aza Kitanakajō Sa-no-2-no-2 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi|Q867534|WD=Q867534|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōmononushi}}, plus seven other deities |社格 = candidate for {{ill|lt=Shikinai Shōsha|Q134917287|WD=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}}, former {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = tradition: {{ill|lt=836|Q24957|WD=Q24957|1=836}} (Jōwa 3) |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=April 15|Q2497|WD=Q2497|1=April 15}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Ishikawa|label=Miwa Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Miwa Shrine''' (みわじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Aza Kitanakajō, {{ill|lt=Tsubata|Q432014|WD=Q432014|1=Tsubata, Ishikawa}}, {{ill|lt=Kahoku District|Q1130942|WD=Q1130942|1=Kahoku District, Ishikawa}}, {{ill|lt=Ishikawa Prefecture|Q131281|WD=Q131281|1=Ishikawa Prefecture}}. It stands in the hills behind {{ill|lt=Tsubata Station|Q3701490|WD=Q3701490|1=Tsubata Station}}. Its {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=former shrine rank|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534}} was {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === It enshrines eight deities: {{ill|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi|Q867534|WD=Q867534|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōmononushi}}, {{ill|lt=Kukurihime|Q11619891|WD=Q11619891|lt=Kukurihime-no-Kami|1=Kukurihime}}, {{ill|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi|Q21391580|WD=Q21391580|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Mikoto|1=Futsunushi}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Kami|1=Takemikazuchi}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane|WD=Q2564508}}, {{ill|lt=Himegami|Q22070227|WD=Q22070227|1=Himegami}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|1=Ukanomitama}}, and {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10935105|lt=Ōyamakui-no-Mikoto|1=Oyamakui no Kami}}. === Origin === Its founding is said to be {{ill|lt=836|Q24957|WD=Q24957|1=836}} ({{ill|lt=Jōwa (Japan)|Q1075605|WD=Q1075605|lt=Jōwa|1=Jōwa (Heian period)}} 3), and it is considered the minor shrine "Miwa Shrine" in Kaga District, {{ill|lt=Kaga Province|Q864166|WD=Q864166|1=Ise Province}}, in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}Another candidate {{ill|lt=ronsha|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} is {{ill|lt=Ishiura Shrine|Q11586806|WD=Q11586806|1=Ishiura Shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kanazawa|Q191130|WD=Q191130|1=Kanazawa}}.. There is a pond in the precincts called Ryūjin-no-Ike. === Access === * 10 minutes on foot from Tsubata Station on {{ill|lt=West Japan Railway Company|Q502125|WD=Q502125|1=West Japan Railway Company}} (JR West) and {{ill|lt=IR Ishikawa Railway|Q11585936|WD=Q11585936|1=IR Ishikawa Railway}} === Notes === {{reflist}} ===References=== * Ishikawa Prefectural Shrine Office (ed.), ''Ishikawa-ken Jinja-shi'', Ishikawa Prefectural Shrine Office, October 26, 1976 === External links === *[https://www.ishikawa-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/j0931/ 三輪神社] (Ishikawa Prefectural Shrine Office) {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Miwa Shrine (Q11357406)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Miwa Shrine (Q11357406)]][[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kaga Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ishikawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kaga Province]] [[Category:石川県の旧村社]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Tsubata Town]] [[Category:Municipal History of Tsubata]] [[Category:Miwa shrines]]

[[JA:三輪神社 (津幡町)]] {{wikidata link|Q11357406}} == Japanese content == {{otheruses|Miwa Shrine in Tsubata, Kahoku District, Ishikawa Prefecture|other Miwa Shrines|{{ill|lt=Miwa Shrine (disambiguation)|UNKNOWN|lt=Miwa Shrine}}}} {{神社 |名称 = Miwa Shrine |画像 = [[画像:MIwajinja-tubata.jpg|250px]]
Torii |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Ishikawa Prefecture|Q131281|qid=Q131281|1=Ishikawa Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kahoku District|Q1130942|qid=Q1130942|1=Kahoku District, Ishikawa}}{{ill|lt=Tsubata|Q432014|qid=Q432014|1=Tsubata, Ishikawa}} Aza Kitanakajō Sa-no-2-no-2 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|qid=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi|Q867534|qid=Q867534|qid=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōmononushi}}, plus seven other deities |社格 = candidate for {{ill|lt=Shikinai Shōsha|Q134917287|qid=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}}, former {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = tradition: {{ill|lt=836|Q24957|qid=Q24957|1=836}} (Jōwa 3) |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=April 15|Q2497|qid=Q2497|1=April 15}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Ishikawa|label=Miwa Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Miwa Shrine''' (みわじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Aza Kitanakajō, {{ill|lt=Tsubata|Q432014|qid=Q432014|1=Tsubata, Ishikawa}}, {{ill|lt=Kahoku District|Q1130942|qid=Q1130942|1=Kahoku District, Ishikawa}}, {{ill|lt=Ishikawa Prefecture|Q131281|qid=Q131281|1=Ishikawa Prefecture}}. It stands in the hills behind {{ill|lt=Tsubata Station|Q3701490|qid=Q3701490|1=Tsubata Station}}. Its {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=former shrine rank|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qid=Q712534}} was {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === It enshrines eight deities: {{ill|qid=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi|Q867534|qid=Q867534|qid=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōmononushi}}, {{ill|lt=Kukurihime|Q11619891|qid=Q11619891|lt=Kukurihime-no-Kami|1=Kukurihime}}, {{ill|qid=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi|Q21391580|qid=Q21391580|qid=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Mikoto|1=Futsunushi}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|qid=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Kami|1=Takemikazuchi}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qid=Q2564508}}, {{ill|lt=Himegami|Q22070227|qid=Q22070227|1=Himegami}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|qid=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|1=Ukanomitama}}, and {{ill|UNKNOWN|qid=Q10935105|lt=Ōyamakui-no-Mikoto|1=Oyamakui no Kami}}. === Origin === Its founding is said to be {{ill|lt=836|Q24957|qid=Q24957|1=836}} ({{ill|lt=Jōwa (Japan)|Q1075605|qid=Q1075605|lt=Jōwa|1=Jōwa (Heian period)}} 3), and it is considered the minor shrine "Miwa Shrine" in Kaga District, {{ill|lt=Kaga Province|Q864166|qid=Q864166|1=Ise Province}}, in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}Another candidate {{ill|lt=ronsha|Q135022904|qid=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} is {{ill|lt=Ishiura Shrine|Q11586806|qid=Q11586806|1=Ishiura Shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kanazawa|Q191130|qid=Q191130|1=Kanazawa}}.. There is a pond in the precincts called Ryūjin-no-Ike. === Access === * 10 minutes on foot from Tsubata Station on {{ill|lt=West Japan Railway Company|Q502125|qid=Q502125|1=West Japan Railway Company}} (JR West) and {{ill|lt=IR Ishikawa Railway|Q11585936|qid=Q11585936|1=IR Ishikawa Railway}} === Notes === {{reflist}} ===References=== * Ishikawa Prefectural Shrine Office (ed.), ''Ishikawa-ken Jinja-shi'', Ishikawa Prefectural Shrine Office, October 26, 1976 === External links === *[https://www.ishikawa-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/j0931/ 三輪神社] (Ishikawa Prefectural Shrine Office) {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Miwa Shrine (Q11357406)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Miwa Shrine (Q11357406)]][[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kaga Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ishikawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kaga Province]] [[Category:石川県の旧村社]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Tsubata Town]] [[Category:Municipal History of Tsubata]] [[Category:Miwa shrines]]

[[JA:三輪神社 (津幡町)]] {{wikidata link|Q11357406}} == Japanese content == {{otheruses|Miwa Shrine in Tsubata, Kahoku District, Ishikawa Prefecture|other Miwa Shrines|{{ill|lt=Miwa Shrine (disambiguation)|UNKNOWN|lt=Miwa Shrine}}}} {{神社 |名称 = Miwa Shrine |画像 = [[画像:MIwajinja-tubata.jpg|250px]]
Torii |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Ishikawa Prefecture|Q131281|qid=Q131281|1=Ishikawa Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kahoku District|Q1130942|qid=Q1130942|1=Kahoku District, Ishikawa}}{{ill|lt=Tsubata|Q432014|qid=Q432014|1=Tsubata, Ishikawa}} Aza Kitanakajō Sa-no-2-no-2 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|qid=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi|Q867534|qid=Q867534|qid=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōmononushi}}, plus seven other deities |社格 = candidate for {{ill|lt=Shikinai Shōsha|Q134917287|qid=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}}, former {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = tradition: {{ill|lt=836|Q24957|qid=Q24957|1=836}} (Jōwa 3) |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=April 15|Q2497|qid=Q2497|1=April 15}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Ishikawa|label=Miwa Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Miwa Shrine''' (みわじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Aza Kitanakajō, {{ill|lt=Tsubata|Q432014|qid=Q432014|1=Tsubata, Ishikawa}}, {{ill|lt=Kahoku District|Q1130942|qid=Q1130942|1=Kahoku District, Ishikawa}}, {{ill|lt=Ishikawa Prefecture|Q131281|qid=Q131281|1=Ishikawa Prefecture}}. It stands in the hills behind {{ill|lt=Tsubata Station|Q3701490|qid=Q3701490|1=Tsubata Station}}. Its {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=former shrine rank|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qid=Q712534}} was {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === It enshrines eight deities: {{ill|qid=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi|Q867534|qid=Q867534|qid=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōmononushi}}, {{ill|lt=Kikurihime|Q11619891|qid=Q11619891|lt=Kikurihime-no-Kami|1=Kikurihime}}, {{ill|qid=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi|Q21391580|qid=Q21391580|qid=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Mikoto|1=Futsunushi}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|qid=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Kami|1=Takemikazuchi}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qid=Q2564508}}, {{ill|lt=Himegami|Q22070227|qid=Q22070227|1=Himegami}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|qid=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|1=Ukanomitama}}, and {{ill|UNKNOWN|qid=Q10935105|lt=Ōyamakui-no-Mikoto|1=Oyamakui no Kami}}. === Origin === Its founding is said to be {{ill|lt=836|Q24957|qid=Q24957|1=836}} ({{ill|lt=Jōwa (Japan)|Q1075605|qid=Q1075605|lt=Jōwa|1=Jōwa (Heian period)}} 3), and it is considered the minor shrine "Miwa Shrine" in Kaga District, {{ill|lt=Kaga Province|Q864166|qid=Q864166|1=Ise Province}}, in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}Another candidate {{ill|lt=ronsha|Q135022904|qid=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} is {{ill|lt=Ishiura Shrine|Q11586806|qid=Q11586806|1=Ishiura Shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kanazawa|Q191130|qid=Q191130|1=Kanazawa}}.. There is a pond in the precincts called Ryūjin-no-Ike. === Access === * 10 minutes on foot from Tsubata Station on {{ill|lt=West Japan Railway Company|Q502125|qid=Q502125|1=West Japan Railway Company}} (JR West) and {{ill|lt=IR Ishikawa Railway|Q11585936|qid=Q11585936|1=IR Ishikawa Railway}} === Notes === {{reflist}} ===References=== * Ishikawa Prefectural Shrine Office (ed.), ''Ishikawa-ken Jinja-shi'', Ishikawa Prefectural Shrine Office, October 26, 1976 === External links === *[https://www.ishikawa-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/j0931/ 三輪神社] (Ishikawa Prefectural Shrine Office) {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Miwa Shrine (Q11357406)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Miwa Shrine (Q11357406)]][[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kaga Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ishikawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kaga Province]] [[Category:石川県の旧村社]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Tsubata Town]] [[Category:Municipal History of Tsubata]] [[Category:Miwa shrines]]
